problem_statement: After the signed approval intake contract and draft PR #2047 evidence exist, a later generic continuation input must still not be interpreted as signed runtime authorization. The system needs a schema-backed no-effect rejection witness that records generic continuation as non-authorizing, binds the signed approval intake, binds closed unmerged PR #2047 evidence, and preserves the path for a future explicit signed approval witness plus separate activation gate.
success_criteria: a deterministic validator emits a signed approval generic-continuation rejection witness from the current signed approval intake contract
the checked-in local rejection witness matches the deterministic produced witness
the schema records GovernanceBlocked and RejectedNoEffect for generic continuation
the witness binds the source signed approval intake id and hash
the witness records closed unmerged draft PR #2047 evidence without treating green checks or closure as approval
the witness records that signed approval remains absent, signature verification remains false, and runtime authorization gate remains unsatisfied

focused tests cover schema compatibility, fixture parity, source intake binding, PR evidence binding, rejected required witness fields, rejected scenarios, approval/signature drift, source/PR drift, runtime authority drift, and CLI JSON output
non_goals: do not record a signed operator approval witness
do not reopen, merge, or alter PR #2047
do not treat green checks as approval
do not execute, dispatch, replan, replay, or rollback
do not add a router, worker, scheduler, connector, or runtime registration path
do not claim live runtime activation
constraints: the rejection witness is local, read-only, deterministic, and reversible
generic continuation is classified as non-authorization even after signed approval intake evidence exists
closed unmerged PR evidence is audit context only and not approval authority
runtime promotion activation remains denied until a separate explicit signed approval witness and activation action are governed
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved because the validator does not normalize or decompose text
evidence_required: schema-backed local signed approval generic-continuation rejection fixture
deterministic validator and produced witness parity
SDLC design and security review artifacts
